snippets.title: Gaussian PDF and log-posterior score by hand
snippets.explanation: Core GNB inference: log prior plus log Gaussian likelihood per feature (one feature here). Argmax over classes — no training loop.

snippets.title: Fit and predict with sklearn GaussianNB
snippets.code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=0)

clf = GaussianNB()
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)

print("class means shape (classes x features):", clf.theta_.shape)
print("class variances shape:", clf.var_.shape)
print("class priors:", clf.class_prior_.round(3))
print("test accuracy:", accuracy_score(y_test, clf.predict(X_test)))
snippets.explanation: theta_ stores per-class per-feature means μ; var_ stores σ². fit estimates priors and Gaussian parameters in one pass.

snippets.title: Inspect per-class Gaussian parameters
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB

X = np.array([[1.0, 10.0], [2.0, 12.0], [8.0, 90.0], [9.0, 95.0]])
y = np.array([0, 0, 1, 1])

clf = GaussianNB().fit(X, y)

for k in clf.classes_:
    idx = y == k
    print(f"class {k}:")
    print("  empirical mean:", X[idx].mean(axis=0))
    print("  fitted theta_:", clf.theta_[list(clf.classes_).index(k)])
    print("  fitted var_:", clf.var_[list(clf.classes_).index(k)])
snippets.explanation: Connects training data statistics to fitted μ and σ². Useful for debugging when predictions surprise you.

snippets.title: Log-posterior scores via predict_log_proba
snippets.code:
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
import numpy as np

X_train = np.array([[0.0], [1.0], [5.0], [6.0]])
y_train = np.array([0, 0, 1, 1])
X_test = np.array([[2.5]])

clf = GaussianNB().fit(X_train, y_train)
log_proba = clf.predict_log_proba(X_test)
print("log P(y=k|x) up to constant:", log_proba)
print("predicted:", clf.predict(X_test))
snippets.explanation: predict_log_proba returns log-posterior scores (normalized in sklearn). Prefer log domain for the same reason as manual GNB.
